dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9304 IH: Juice Access Improvement Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Thompson of Pennsylvania (for himself and Mr. Soto) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce

form.official-title: To require that juice be the default benefit under certain WIC food packages.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Juice Access Improvement Act.

section.header: Juice as default benefit under certain WIC food packages

subsection.text: In carrying out the special supplemental nutrition program for women, infants, and children under section 17 of the Child Nutrition Act of 1966 (42 U.S.C. 1786), the Secretary shall, with respect to a covered Food Package, require State agencies to—

paragraph.text: establish juice as the default benefit; and

authorize that a $3 cash-value voucher substitution for juice (to be adjusted annually for inflation consistent with the annual inflation adjustments made to cash-value voucher values for women and children) be available upon request by a participant in such program.

subsection.text: The Secretary shall issue or revise such rules as are necessary to carry out this section, including revision of paragraph (e)(10) of section 246.10 of title 7, Code of Federal Regulations and table 2 relating to such paragraph (as in effect on the date of the enactment of this section).

paragraph.text: The term cash-value voucher has the meaning given the term in section 246.2 of title 7, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on the date of the enactment of this section).
The term covered Food Package means the following food packages under section 246.10(e) of title 7, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on the date of the enactment of this section):

subparagraph.text: Food Package V—Pregnant and partially (mostly) breastfeeding women.

paragraph.text: The term juice means juice meeting the minimum requirements and specifications for supplemental foods under table 4 to paragraph (e)(12) of section 246.10 of title 7, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on the date of the enactment of this section).
The term Secretary means the Secretary of Agriculture.
